title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum prices rose to 3644.45 USD/T on April 16, 2026, up 0.67% from the previous day, with UK futures climbing above $3,650 per tonne amid supply shortfall concerns. Over the past month, prices have risen 8.16%, and are up 52.93% year-over-year. Forecasts indicate trading at 3558.07 USD/Tonne by quarter-end.

title: Live Aluminum Prices Today
summary: Global aluminum prices advanced 0.74% to $3,611.01/ton, driven by U.S. factors and Chinese spot at ¥24,620/ton. Recent analysis notes flat prices at $3,461.28/Ton amid supply shocks from Iranian strikes on smelters and Strait of Hormuz closure, projecting a 570,000-tonne deficit.

title: Canada Tightens Supply, Raises ESG Aluminum Prices — The U.S. ...
summary: Canada is tightening ESG aluminum supply and raising prices, with the U.S. reportedly being sidelined first in this major shift. The video covers breaking news on these supply restrictions amid broader market dynamics.

title: Aluminium Market Data
summary: Global aluminium producers face uncertainty over power supplies due to elevated oil and gas prices from the Strait of Hormuz closure, affecting 20% of global oil and LNG flows. This impacts market movements and aluminium pricing trends.

title: Aluminum Shortages About To Begin As Supply Chain Wrecked
summary: Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA), the Gulf's largest aluminum producer, halted operations at its Al Taweelah smelter following Iranian missile and drone strikes. EGA invoked force majeure clauses to suspend some deliveries after the damage, potentially leading to aluminum shortages and rising prices.

title: Century Aluminum sees higher demand, restarts capacity, and plans 750,000-tonne smelter
summary: Century Aluminum is restarting capacity due to higher demand and planning a 750,000-tonne smelter. Its total production capacity stands at about 770,000 tonnes per year, with 638,000 tonnes of primary aluminum produced in 2025.

title: Aluminium deficit will support prices in 2026
summary: China's aluminum output is nearing its self-imposed 45 million tonne capacity cap, reducing net exports by 700kt year-to-date and tightening markets outside China. This capacity limit, introduced in 2017, aims to curb oversupply and emissions, supporting higher prices in 2026.

title: Aluminum DIVERTED to Europe as Factories COLLAPSE
summary: Canadian aluminum supplies are being diverted to Europe amid U.S. factory collapses, straining U.S. supply chains. The video discusses impacts from Trump's policies on aluminum availability.

title: Powering Up American Aluminum: A Roadmap for Next Generation
summary: Domestic primary aluminum production in the U.S. has declined over recent decades, while recycled secondary aluminum production reached record levels in 2024. The article outlines a roadmap to boost American aluminum capabilities.

title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
summary: Domestic raw steel production in the US reached 1,843,000 net tons for the week ending April 11, 2026, with a capability utilization rate of 79.8 percent. Production breakdown by districts includes North East: 125k tons, Great Lakes: 515k tons, Midwest: 311k tons, Southern: 830k tons, and Western: 62k tons.

title: US crude steel output nears 80% capacity utilisation
summary: US crude steel production increased for the fourth consecutive week, bringing capacity utilization close to 80 percent. This reflects ongoing recovery and improved operational rates in the domestic steel sector.

title: [SMM Survey] Construction Steel Production Increased Slightly, and Mill Inventories Continued to Decline
summary: Construction steel production saw a slight increase due to more operating days compared to the previous period. This led to growth in wire rod supply, while mill inventories continued to decline.

title: Steel production in China fell by 4.6% y/y in Q1
summary: China's steel production declined by 4.6% year-over-year in the first quarter. This update is part of ongoing steel market insights covering production trends and capacity utilization.

title: Danieli Signs Agreement with Marcegaglia for New Green Steelmaking Facility
summary: Danieli announced an agreement with Marcegaglia to construct a new green steelmaking and flat rolling facility in Fos-sur-Mer, France. This project emphasizes sustainable steel production technologies. It represents a key innovation in reducing the environmental impact of steel manufacturing.[5]

title: Indo-Aus Team Shows Farm Waste Can Cut Steel Emissions
source: Steel on the Net / Northern Miner
summary: An India-Australia research partnership tested agricultural waste like rice husk pellets as a coal substitute in steel production. This innovation could reduce carbon dioxide emissions from India's steel industry by up to 50 percent. It provides a sustainable pathway for decarbonizing steel manufacturing.[2]

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent operational challenges at the site.

title: Ivanhoe Mines Announces Updated, Independent Study Results for the Kamoa-Kakula Copper Complex
summary: Ivanhoe Mines has revised production guidance for the Kamoa-Kakula Copper Complex downward to 290,000 to 330,000 tonnes of copper anodes in 2026 and 380,000 to 420,000 tonnes in 2027 due to increased focus on development activities and conservative underground advance rates. The complex is expected to ramp up to over 500,000 tonnes annually from 2028 onwards, supported by a new mineral resource estimate and mine plan for Phases 1, 2, and 3 concentrators processing 17 million tonnes per annum over 25 years.

title: Global Copper Output Decline Sparks Industrial Supply Chain Crisis
summary: The global copper market faces a significant output decline, threatening to reshape industrial supply chains worldwide. This reduction in production is sparking concerns over potential shortages and disruptions in various sectors reliant on copper.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Global nickel prices edged marginally lower to $18,137.36/ton, with Chinese markets at ¥123,661/ton, reflecting a 0.28% dip. Nickel prices hold a strict flatline, maintaining a tight global trading range supported by Indonesia's reduced ore production quotas alleviating oversupply fears. Additional strength comes from recovering Chinese EV battery and stainless steel demand, though upside is capped by tiered Indonesian royalty rates.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel rose to 18,205 USD/T on April 16, 2026, up 0.30% from the previous day, hitting an over two-month high due to cost pressures and supply constraints. Over the past month, prices have risen 5.51%, with futures reaching around $18,200 per tonne. Prices have stabilized in the $17,000–$17,400 range amid tighter Indonesian quotas, though gains are capped by elevated global inventories and a projected 2026 surplus.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: The world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, PT Weda Bay Nickel, has been notified to cut its ore production quota to 12 million tons this year from 42 million tons in 2025. This move aims to boost global prices of nickel, Indonesia's key export commodity. Nickel prices climbed last month following Indonesia's plans to reduce mine output.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures rose to around $18,200 per tonne, reaching an over two-month high due to cost pressures and supply constraints from Indonesia's quota cuts and revised nickel ore pricing formula. Jakarta signaled continued production discipline for 2026 with output quotas of 190–200 million tons. Supply strain in Indonesia’s HPAL sector and steady battery demand are supporting elevated nickel prices.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials have developed a carbon-free, energy-saving method for nickel extraction using hydrogen plasma from low-grade ores. This one-step process eliminates CO2 emissions, reduces energy use by up to 18 percent when powered by renewables, and makes extraction more cost-effective. The team aims to scale it for industrial applications.
